forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@
+---
+depends_on:
+- 01KWMS0CJ2DMWM46AB5JE50TAR
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8780'
+title: 'MCPServer core: async discovery, pagination to exhaustion, readiness states'
+---
+## What
+Create `Sources/FoundationModelsMCP/MCPServer.swift` (actor): wraps one `MCP.Client` (caller owns transport setup). Async connect + `listTools()` **following `nextCursor` to exhaustion** (tools/list is paginated â€” a one-page read silently truncates). Readiness state machine: `connecting / ready / faulted`, exposed as an async-awaitable property. Once ready, maps each `MCP.Tool` into an `MCPTool` and vends `[MCPTool]` / `[any FoundationModels.Tool]`. Establish a **stable `ServerIdentity`** value (host-supplied name or derived, documented) that survives reconnects.
+
+- [ ] Actor wrapping MCP.Client, async connect + full paginated discovery
+- [ ] Readiness state machine (connecting/ready/faulted)
+- [ ] Vend [MCPTool] / [any Tool]
+- [ ] Stable ServerIdentity
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] With a 3-page scripted tools/list, all pages' tools are discovered (no truncation)
+- [ ] State transitions connectingâ†’ready observed; faulted on scripted connect failure
+- [ ] ServerIdentity is identical before and after a scripted reconnect
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `Tests/FoundationModelsMCPTests/MCPServerDiscoveryTests.swift`: paginated discovery completeness, state transitions, identity stability (uses ScriptedServer/MockClient)
+- [ ] `swift test --filter MCPServerDiscovery` green
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` â€” write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file
